metaDescription: Proactive tax planning for UK businesses and directors. We plan all year round, not just at the deadline, so you keep more of your profit and never miss a date.
title: Proactive Tax Planning That Looks Ahead
intro: Most accountants tell you what your tax bill is after the year has closed, when nothing can be changed. We work the other way round — planning ahead all year so you keep more of your profit legitimately and meet every deadline without a single surprise.
highlights: Tax reviewed all year, not just filed at the deadline
A pre-year-end checklist while there is still time to act
Salary and dividend planning for director-shareholders
Allowances and reliefs used before they are lost
Capital spend and disposals timed to your advantage
Every deadline diarised and kept comfortably ahead

sections.heading: Planning before the year end, not paperwork after it
body: There is a world of difference between an accountant who records the past and one who helps shape what is coming. Filing a return is the easy part; the value sits in the months beforehand, when there is still time to make a decision that changes the outcome.

We treat tax as a year-round conversation rather than an annual scramble. By the time your year end arrives, the thinking has already been done — so the bill is the result of a plan, not a shock you find out about in January.
This is general guidance rather than advice tailored to your own circumstances, which is exactly what we work through with you in person.

sections.heading: A pre-year-end review, while there is still time to act
body: Many of the most useful tax decisions have to be made before your accounting period closes. Once the year is over, the window quietly shuts. So we build in a review ahead of your year end, working through a clear checklist of the things still within your control.
It is a calm, deliberate look at your position rather than a last-minute reaction — the difference between steering and simply reporting where you ended up.
bullets: How profit is taxed and how it is best drawn from the business
Whether allowances and reliefs are on track to be used in full
The timing of income, costs and any planned disposals
Pension contributions as part of the wider picture

sections.heading: How you pay yourself matters
body: If you own and run your company, the way you take money out makes a real difference to what you keep. We plan the mix of salary and dividends around your wider income, making use of allowances such as the £500 dividend allowance and factoring pension contributions into the plan rather than bolting them on at the end.

There is rarely a single right answer that fits everyone, because the best blend depends on your circumstances. That is the point of planning it properly, together, instead of defaulting to a rule of thumb. Our tax planning service covers the detail and the figures behind these decisions.

sections.heading: Using allowances and timing before they slip away
body: Reliefs you do not claim in time are simply lost, and across a year that adds up. When your business invests in equipment, the right capital allowances can give valuable relief in the year you spend — the Annual Investment Allowance covers up to £1,000,000 of qualifying spend, and full expensing offers further relief on qualifying new plant and machinery for companies.

Timing is just as important as the claim itself. Bringing a purchase forward, or planning when income and capital disposals fall, can change the tax due. We look at these levers in good time so a decision is made on purpose rather than by accident.
bullets: Capital spend timed to make the most of available allowances
Income and capital disposals planned rather than left to chance
Reliefs claimed in full while the window is still open

sections.heading: Deadlines kept, surprises removed
body: Tax penalties are automatic and entirely avoidable, so we track every date for you and work backwards from it. Some deadlines are tighter than people expect — Capital Gains Tax on a UK residential property, for instance, must be reported and paid within 60 days of completion, which catches a lot of landlords out.

Knowing what is due, and when, well in advance also means you can plan the cash rather than find it in a hurry. Our approach joins up with our VAT and business advisory services, and suits owners across sectors such as contractors and landlords who have several moving parts to keep aligned.

faqs.question: How is proactive tax planning different from just filing my return?
faqs.answer: Filing records what already happened. Proactive tax planning happens before your year end, while you can still influence the result — reviewing how profit is taxed and drawn, and which allowances apply. By the time the return is due, the thinking is already done, so there are no surprises.

faqs.question: When should we be having tax planning conversations?
faqs.answer: Throughout the year, with a focused review before your accounting period closes. Many of the most useful decisions have to be made before the year end, so we build in a pre-year-end checklist while the things within your control are still open to change.

faqs.question: Can planning really help me keep more of my profit?
faqs.answer: Often, yes — legitimately. Making full use of allowances such as the dividend allowance and capital allowances, planning the salary and dividend mix for director-shareholders and timing income or disposals can all affect the tax due. This is general guidance; we tailor it to you.

faqs.question: What happens if a deadline is tight, like Capital Gains Tax on property?
faqs.answer: We track every date and warn you well ahead. Some are short — Capital Gains Tax on a UK residential property disposal must be reported and paid within 60 days of completion, for example — so we plan around them rather than reacting once the clock is already running.

metaDescription: A business advisory partnership for ambitious UK firms. We turn your numbers into confident decisions, like a finance director without the full-time cost.
title: An Advisory & Growth Partnership
intro: Compliance keeps you on the right side of the rules. It does not, on its own, help you grow. Our advisory partnership goes further — turning your numbers into confident decisions on pricing, cash flow and growth, so you have a finance partner in your corner rather than just a service that files your returns.
highlights: A finance partner, not just a compliance service
Management accounts and the KPIs that matter to you
Cash-flow forecasting and budgeting that look ahead
Virtual finance director input for the bigger calls
Funding and investor readiness when you need it
Pricing, margin and benchmarking that lift profit

sections.heading: From keeping score to shaping the result
body: Every business needs its compliance done well, but that work looks backwards by design — it tells you what already happened. The far more valuable question is what happens next, and that is where a real advisory partnership earns its place.

We work alongside you, using your figures to inform the decisions in front of you rather than just the ones already behind you. The relationship is closer to having someone in your corner than handing work over and waiting for the verdict.

sections.heading: Numbers that answer real questions
body: A set of year-end accounts arrives far too late to change anything. Regular management accounts give you the picture while it is still useful — and, just as importantly, focus on the handful of measures that genuinely move your business rather than burying the signal in detail.

We agree the KPIs that matter to you and report against them in plain English, so every update answers a question you actually care about instead of leaving you to interpret a spreadsheet alone. Our business advisory service covers this in full.
bullets: Management accounts produced regularly, not once a year
KPIs chosen around what drives your business
A plain-English read on the numbers, not just a report

sections.heading: Seeing cash and decisions before they arrive
body: Profitable businesses still come unstuck when they run short of cash, because profit and cash are not the same thing. A forward view of your cash flow shows what is coming so you can act before a squeeze becomes a problem rather than after.

Pairing a budget with that forecast lets you test the big moves on paper first — a key hire, a price change, a quieter quarter — so you commit with your eyes open. It turns the major decisions into something you can weigh up calmly rather than gamble on.
bullets: Cash-flow forecasting that looks weeks and months ahead
Budgets and targets to plan against
The big decisions tested before you commit to them

sections.heading: A finance director, without the full-time cost
body: Plenty of growing businesses need finance leadership long before they are ready to employ a finance director. Virtual finance director support gives you that senior input exactly when you need it, on terms that suit your stage.

It means a seasoned head for the calls that carry weight — pricing, investment, funding and strategy — backed by the regular numbers we already prepare for you. You get the judgement of an experienced FD without the salary, and without waiting until you can justify the hire.

sections.heading: Funding, pricing and the path to growth
body: When you approach a lender or an investor, the quality of your numbers shapes the outcome. We help you get funding and investor ready — with the forecasts and clear management information they expect — so you walk in prepared rather than hoping the figures hold up.

Day to day, small changes to pricing and margin often do more for profit than chasing extra sales. Through benchmarking, pricing reviews and margin analysis we look at where you genuinely make money and where you quietly lose it. This partnership sits naturally alongside our tax planning service and suits startups and scaling businesses with real ambition.
bullets: Funding and investor readiness, including forecasts and plans
Pricing and margin analysis to keep more of the value you create

faqs.question: How is an advisory partnership different from standard accountancy?
faqs.answer: Standard compliance keeps you on the right side of the rules and looks backwards at what already happened. An advisory partnership looks forward — using your numbers to help you decide on pricing, cash flow, funding and growth, so you have a finance partner rather than just a filing service.

faqs.question: What does a virtual finance director actually do for us?
faqs.answer: A virtual FD gives you senior finance input without the cost of a full-time hire. In practice that means support with the bigger decisions — pricing, budgeting, funding, investment and strategy — backed by the regular management information we prepare, on terms that suit your stage.

faqs.question: Why does cash-flow forecasting matter if we are profitable?
faqs.answer: Because profit and cash are not the same thing, and profitable businesses can still run short of cash. A forward-looking forecast shows what is coming, so you can plan around tax payments, hires and quieter periods before they cause a problem rather than reacting once they hit.

faqs.question: Is this suitable for an early-stage or growing business?
faqs.answer: Yes — it suits startups and scaling businesses especially well, because those are the stages where good decisions compound. We scale the input to where you are, from management accounts and forecasts through to funding readiness and virtual finance director support as you grow.
